strategy_description: Fade IV residual vs volume-WLS quadratic smile (m_t=log(K/S)/sqrt(T)); hydrogel spread capture; vega-scaled size. RESID_ENTER=0.012, MAX_Q=18.
Parameter sweep: no hydrogel, stricter thresholds (ENTER=0.02), spread filter MAX_SPREAD=10, aggressive BS delta hedge on extract vs portfolio option delta.
Grid: spread filter on vouchers (<=8), RESID_ENTER=0.015/EXIT=0.005, MAX_Q=12; hydrogel only when spread>=14 with edge 4 and size 5 (lighter than v0).

Residual fade still based on IV minus volume-WLS smile, but only trade liquid strikes VEV_5000..VEV_5500 and fit smile on those same strikes; RESID_ENTER=0.014, RESID_EXIT=0.004, MAX_SPREAD=8, MAX_Q=12. Hydrogel passive quoting unchanged.

Parameter sweep from v3: trade/fitting set widened to VEV_5000..VEV_5500 plus VEV_5200/5300/5400 emphasis retained, RESID_ENTER=0.013, RESID_EXIT=0.0045, MAX_SPREAD=9. Same residual-fade thesis and hydrogel passive leg.
From v3: fit WLS on all ten VEVs; trade 5000–5500; entry spread <=6, RESID 0.0135/0.005. PnL 94 — full-chain fit + 5000 leg hurt day 0 vs v3; superseded by v7.
Drop VEV_5000 from trading; test full-chain fit (all VEV) vs trade 5100–5500. Full-chain refit was worse than v3; dropped for v7.
From v3: self-contained 5-strike WLS on trade strip VEV_5100..VEV_5500 only (omit 5000); same parameters as v3. Superseded by v8 on total PnL.

Parent v7: same 5-strike WLS strip 5100–5500. Add EMA (α=0.12) on raw IV residual per strike for entry signal; exits on raw resid; min top-of-book volume sum 28 for new entries; RESID_ENTER=0.0145, RESID_EXIT=0.0045. Best total in this series (so far).
Parent v8: WLS weights = (1+book)^1.25 * (1+0.002*vega) (vega-conviction smile); EMA α=0.10; RESID 0.014/0.004; min book 24. Grid sweep: worse than v8 — keep linear 1+vol WLS.
Parent v8: mild EMA/threshold sweep — α=0.11, RESID_ENTER=0.014, RESID_EXIT=0.0042, min book 26. Worse than v8; v8 params remain best.
Parent v8: same residual-fade voucher logic; hydrogel risk wind-down (reduce quote size and widen edge after timestamp>=700000). Degraded total vs v8; keep v8 as best.

Parent v8: add innovation gate on new entries — only fade when EMA(residual) still moves in the mispricing direction (step vs prior EMA) by INNOV_MIN=0.0004; first tick per strike unchanged. Worse than v8 (11013 vs 13478); v8 remains best.

Family10 cadence-fast baseline (smile_quadratic_logm_wls): refit every 100 timestamps. Same risk/execution guardrails as v8. fills: rows=4843, abs_qty=18099, key symbols HYDROGEL_PACK=1207 rows, VEV_5400=823, VEV_5500=267, VEV_5300=121

Family10 cadence-slow: refit every 500 timestamps. Same guardrails; identical output to v13 in this environment. fills: rows=4843, abs_qty=18099, key symbols HYDROGEL_PACK=1207 rows, VEV_5400=823, VEV_5500=267, VEV_5300=121

Family10 cadence-conditional: refit every 300 timestamps only when |EMA residual| exceeds gate. Same guardrails; identical output to v13/v14 in this environment. fills: rows=4843, abs_qty=18099, key symbols HYDROGEL_PACK=1207 rows, VEV_5400=823, VEV_5500=267, VEV_5300=121

Adjusted non-time variant attempt (event-triggered refit + stale timeout) under family10 quadratic_logm_wls. Output remained identical to v8/v13-v15; effectively still duplicate/no-op in this branch. fills: rows=4843, abs_qty=18099, HYDROGEL_PACK=1207 rows, VEV_5400=823, VEV_5500=267

Analysis-driven underlying-response gate: only open voucher fades on large |dS| shocks (>=2 ticks) and only for symbols with strong dV~dS response rank (5200/5300/5400 first, 5100 optional). Kept family smile_quadratic_logm_wls + guardrails; reduced voucher activity but lower total PnL than v8.

strategy_description: Parent v8: same IV-residual fade. Add analysis-derived vega-scaled size multipliers by strike (5200/5300/5400 up, 5500 down) from underlying_to_voucher_response score map. Integer q rounding; backtest PnL and tradeHistory match v8 exactly.
